title: A simple mortality prediction model for sepsis patients in intensive care

sample_size.source_quote: Therefore, the total number of patients included in the study was 1984.

mortality_predictors.source_quote: SAPS-3, on ICU admission, had an AUC of 0.75 (95% CI 0.72–0.77).
SOFA score upon ICU admission had an AUC of 0.67 (95% CI 0.64–0.70).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Six-parameter model (age, immunosuppression, GCS, body temperature, CRP, bilirubin)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Backward stepwise multivariate logistic regression and ROC curve analysis (AUC)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: age, immunosuppression, Glasgow Coma Scale, body temperature, C-reactive protein, bilirubin
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Our six-parameter model had an AUC of 0.72 (95% CI 0.69–0.75) with no difference in predictive strength compared to SAPS-3 (p = 0.071).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Six-parameter model (age, immunosuppression, GCS, body temperature, CRP, bilirubin) - cross-validated
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: age, immunosuppression, Glasgow Coma Scale, body temperature, C-reactive protein, bilirubin
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Internal validation of the six-parameter model was undertaken through five-fold cross-validation with 50 repeats, which yielded an AUC of 0.71 (95% CI 0.66–0.76).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: age, GCS, body temperature, CRP, bilirubin
mortality_predictors.source_quote: A six-parameter model was found with odds ratios (OR) of more than one for age, immunosuppression, CRP and bilirubin and OR of less than one for GCS score and body temperature.
